key_points: Grouped data: Data arranged in class intervals with frequencies.
Cumulative frequency: sum of all frequencies up to a class.
Measures of central tendency for grouped data: Mean, Median, Mode.
Mean deviation: Average of absolute deviations from mean or median.
Graphs: Histogram, Frequency Polygon, Less-than/Often cumulative frequency curve.
formulas: Mean (assumed mean method) = a + [Σf·d / Σf] × h, where d = (x − a)/h
Mode (grouped) = L + [(f1 − f0)/(2f1 − f0 − f2)] × h
Mean deviation about mean = Σ|x − mean| / n
Step deviation method simplifies calculations for large numbers: d = (x − a)/h
examples: Marks of students: 0–10→3, 10–20→7, 20–30→12, 30–40→15, 40–50→13 → Find mean using assumed mean 25
Mean deviation about median: Data 4,6,8,10,12 → Median = 8 → Deviations |4−8|, |6−8|, |8−8|, |10−8|, |12−8| → MD = 4
Cumulative frequency graph: Use upper class boundaries and cumulative frequency to estimate median
exam_tips: Always check n = Σf before calculating median or mean.
For grouped data, use class marks as representative values.
Identify correct median class using n/2-th item in cumulative frequency.
Use step deviation method to avoid large multiplication errors.
When drawing graphs, mark axes clearly and scale properly.
